title: Achyrophorus brasiliensis
intro: Achyrophorus brasiliensis, also known as "chega" or "bicho de pé", is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Apiaceae, commonly referred to as a type of wild carrot or parsnip. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Achyrophorus brasiliensis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Achyrophorus brasiliensis is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and syrups.
taxonomy: Achyrophorus brasiliensis belongs to the family Amaranthaceae. It is a species of flowering plant.
